Interface Location

All Known Implementing Classes:
Home, HomeLocation, Loc, OutsideLocation
public interface Location
Represents a location within the Sims world.

A Location defines the contract for places that Sims can occupy and interact with. Implementing classes should provide details about available activities, Sims present at the location, and movement logic.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    List<Activity>
    getActivity()
    Retrieves the list of activities available in this context.
    List<Sim>
    getLocSimList()
    Retrieves the list of Sims currently located in this context.
    String
    getName()
    Retrieves the name of this entity.
    void
    moveTo(Sim sim)
    Moves the specified Sim into this location.
    void
    removeSim(Sim sim)
    Removes the specified Sim from this location.

  • Method Details

    • getActivity

      List<Activity> getActivity()
      Retrieves the list of activities available in this context.

      Implementations of this method should return all Activity objects currently associated with the entity (e.g., a HomeLocation). Each activity represents an action a Sim can perform, such as eating, sleeping, or socializing, and is tied to a specific need category.

      Usage Example:

      List activities = livingRoom.getActivity();
for (Activity activity : activities) {
    System.out.println(activity.getName());
}

      The returned list may be empty if no activities are currently available, or it may grow as upgrades are added to the location. This provides a flexible way to query and interact with the Sim’s environment.

      Returns:
      a list of Activity objects available in this entity

    • getLocSimList

      List<Sim> getLocSimList()
      Retrieves the list of Sims currently located in this context.

      Implementations of this method should return all Sim objects that are present in the associated location (e.g., a HomeLocation). This allows the game to track which Sims are interacting within a space at any given time.

      Usage Example:

      List simsInLivingRoom = livingRoom.getLocSimList();
for (Sim sim : simsInLivingRoom) {
    System.out.println(sim.getName() + " is here.");
}

      The returned list may be empty if no Sims are currently in the location. It can grow or shrink dynamically as Sims move between locations.

      Returns:
      a list of Sim objects currently in this location

    • removeSim

      void removeSim(Sim sim)
      Removes the specified Sim from this location.

      Implementations of this method should update the internal list of Sims (e.g., locSimList) by removing the given Sim object. This reflects the Sim leaving the location, ensuring that the list accurately represents which Sims are currently present.

      Usage Example:

      livingRoom.removeSim(alice);
// Alice is no longer tracked as being in the living room

      If the specified Sim is not present in the location, the method should have no effect. This provides safe handling for cases where removal is attempted on a Sim not currently in the list.

      Parameters:
      sim - the Sim to remove from this location

    • getName

      String getName()
      Retrieves the name of this entity.

      Implementations of this method should return the identifying name associated with the object (e.g., a Sim, HomeLocation, or Activity). The name is typically used for display, personalization, or referencing the entity within the game.

      Usage Example:

      String simName = sim.getName();
System.out.println("Sim's name: " + simName);
      Returns:
      the name of this entity as a String

    • moveTo

      void moveTo(Sim sim)
      Moves the specified Sim into this location.

      Implementations of this method should update the internal list of Sims (e.g., locSimList) by adding the given Sim object. This reflects the Sim entering the location, ensuring that the list accurately represents which Sims are currently present.

      Usage Example:

      livingRoom.moveTo(alice);
// Alice is now tracked as being in the living room

      If the specified Sim is already present in the location, the method should avoid duplication. This provides safe handling for cases where multiple move attempts are made for the same Sim.

      Parameters:
      sim - the Sim to move into this location
